element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Introductory boss Guggler teaches basic evasion and attack timing
Each fighter's unique abilities (air jumps, charges, slaps) demonstrated by playable roster
Kangaroo uses tail balancing and leg flinging requiring new defensive strategies
Dinosaur's ground shock waves require spacing management beyond direct combat
Genie transforms into multiple animal forms (El Ballz, Spike, Byte Viper) requiring adaptive strategy
Ultimate boss can disassemble and move independently; two-phase fight with reassembly comeback

typical_relief_triggers: Defeating a boss progresses to next difficulty tier
Belt reward system provides visual progression feedback
Unlocking boss characters after defeating them
typical_tension_triggers: Each new boss introduces unique mechanical gimmick
Jester's two-phase final confrontation with health recovery
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Tournament-based escalation with incremental mechanical complexity per boss; four difficulty levels with each boss encounter introducing new challenge archetype

difficulty_curve.early_game: Guggler (ostrich boss) introduces basic pattern recognition with pecking and beak tosses; establishes pacing baseline
difficulty_curve.mid_game: T-Wrecks (dinosaur) escalates with area-denial mechanics (ground shock waves); Lamprey (genie) introduces transformation switching requiring adaptive combat responses
difficulty_curve.late_game: Jester (final boss) combines all previous mechanics with disassembly gimmick and two-phase structure; highest damage output requiring health management
notable_spikes: T-Wrecks introduces environmental hazard (shock waves) as first non-direct combat threat
Lamprey's transformation system breaks single-strategy approaches
Jester's reassembly phase after first defeat signals escalated stakes

notable_level_patterns.description: Each boss sequentially introduces one new mechanical archetype: Guggler (basic pattern), Bounder (multi-phase mobility), T-Wrecks (area denial), Lamprey (form-shifting), Jester (disassembly/reassembly). Progression gates new mechanics one-per-encounter.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Tournament mode: 5 boss encounters spanning 21 total matches

notable_level_patterns.description: Each boss defeat awards colored belt (Red, Green, Blue, Black, then Jester unlock). Belts modify fighter appearance/coloring, creating visual progression markers and replayability incentive
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Post-Guggler victory triggers Red Belt cosmetic

notable_level_patterns.description: 8 playable fighters with distinct mechanical identities (Boomer: air jumps/tricks; Crusher: charging; Divine: spinning grace; Kronk: club-based hitting; Tsunami: leap attacks; Turbo: gliding; Yoko: slapping gang-up; Bruiser: raw power)
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Fighter selection menu demonstrates balanced archetype coverage

sources_used: tcrf - Ballz (SNES) - The Cutting Room Floor
